channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Lighting on Wyatt close dark area next to the sheltered housing, 9th April
item.description:
Cars turning up late at night. Eating and drinking and leaving rubbish! Very bad lighting next to the small car park facing the river. Very dark and it's really not very safe for women in the area walking home from college and university or work in the darker evenings . Lighting needs to be improved in that particular spot. We have been asking for over twenty years. Please can you make it a pleasant and safe environment for everyone to live in.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wyatt Close, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken pavements filled with tarmac instead of like for like paving slabs., 9th April
item.description:
Unused broken unsightly rusty bicycle racks at the top of the river walk way on Wyatt close. They have been there for 33 years nobody uses them. Weds are surrounding the bicycle racks. Children have had accidents on them swinging on the bars !!! They are dangerous! They are on Wyatt close near number 9 Wyatt close. Please don't mistake with the farm bicycle racks. Please don't remove the ones next to the farm!!! Overhanging trees Unkempt area overspills of the bins especially when the only bin is overflowing! Fox squirrel and dog mess.all over Wyatt close floor. Missing paving slabs 15 years ago it was agreed to replace the broken missing slabs , with like for like??? They then filled it with tarmac??? This wasn't in the agreement. The river Thames walkway on Wyatt close is very very neglected. I have cleaned the area for twenty years and sadly I have had an operation and I am unable to clean and sweep the rotting leaves, rubbish and beers cans dirty nappies and coffee cups and polystyrene boxes from the floor of Wyatt close SE16 5Ul Also the dog mess that people leave. We want to really look after Wyatt close and I can do some volunteering for the gardening side and maybe some hanging baskets We want to make the area of Wyatt close a clean and beautiful place to live with the help of Southwark council and a few volunteers. Please replace the missing slabs and the six missing trees. I look forward to your reply.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thames Path, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: LED Street lights, 9th April
item.description:
The street Light is excessively bright. Over a duration of time this is becoming apparent . There is no charging facilities. I have contacted the local council and the local authority. The street light is excessively bright.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balls Pond Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street Light - Equipment damaged, pole leaning

item.title: Flytipping, 9th April
item.description:
Dumped by the person who lives number 56 Granville Avenue who does this regularly waiting for me to leave before dumping another item but I clearly caught him on my ring doorbell.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Avenue, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branch, 9th April
item.description:
Tree branch hit me in forehead have small cut and little bruised. Dangerous overhanging at the front of Ashburn court bromley br1 4bf. Have a picture of my injury

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lake Avenue, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Open-top bin, 8th April
item.description:
We live opposite the green space on Belvedere road and note a workman often attends to litter pick from the area surrounding the bin, however ultimately the problem persists and the litter in the bin is reachable by animals (I assume foxes or magpies), with the contents being carried out of the bin and onto the neighbouring green area. It's a mild frustration and eye sore, but I just wondered if a closed top bin could be installed to replace the existing bin and hopefully remove the issue altogether, while saving labour hours for the street cleansing team! Appreciate the consideration.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing black bins - please collect, 8th April
item.description:
Bins

item.title: Trees damaged, 8th April
item.description:
Tree on communal garden at the back of St Marks House, Queens Row side. Possibly vandalism and at the front of St Marks House the of the post of a new tree the string is broken and need securing. Its in corner of communal Garden near the bloke and fence.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Phelp Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: 143-151 High St Penge, bare plywood builders' hoardings securing the fire damaged Poundland store - about 4 Sq m of red outline spray paint tags, 8th April
item.description:
143-151 High St Penge, bare plywood builders' hoardings securing the fire damaged Poundland store - about 4 Sq m of red outline spray paint tags This is compounding the detrimental visual impact of the fire damaged shop as a place of crime and lawlessness.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 143-151, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping of waste by neighbour in skip, 8th April
item.description:
Household and electrical waste has been dumped in our skip on two nights running. The first night the person was caught by another neighbour filling their skip at no. 4 Lubbock Road and was told to remove it, around 7pm. They subsequently took some of the items to my skip on Porrington Close. They returned this evening sometime between 6.20pm and 8.20pm to dump more waste. Some of the items will have to be removed from the skip for special disposal such as electrical items, 5 ft LED tube lights, a tattoo gun and inks. Some of the packaging has the owner's name and address on. They live in Lower Camden. I can no longer use the skip as it has been filled with this person's waste, and the skip company will refuse to take it if I don't remove the prohibited items.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Porrington Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: South Aldershot, 8th April
item.description:
